$$ \begin{aligned} \text{expected loss} &= \text{bias}^2+\text{variance}+\text{noise} \\ \text {bias}^{2} &=\int\left\{\mathbb{E}_{\mathcal{D}}[y(\mathbf{x} ; \mathcal{D})]-h(\mathbf{x})\right\}^{2} p(\mathbf{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathbf{x} \\
\text { variance } &=\int \mathbb{E}_{\mathcal{D}}\left[\left\{y(\mathbf{x} ; \mathcal{D})-\mathbb{E}_{\mathcal{D}}[y(\mathbf{x} ; \mathcal{D})]\right\}^{2}\right] p(\mathbf{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathbf{x} \\
\text { noise } &=\int\{h(\mathbf{x})-t\}^{2} p(\mathbf{x}, t) \mathrm{d} \mathbf{x} \end{aligned} $$
